content.problemStatement:
Suppose you have an array BBB of size MMM.

You may perform the following operation on it:


Choose an index iii such that 1≤i≤M1 \le i \le M1≤i≤M and subtract 111 from BiB_iBi​.

This operation is allowed only if Bi>0B_i > 0Bi​>0.
Define f(B)f(B)f(B) as the **minimum** number of operations needed to change the MEX†^\dagger† of the array BBB.

If it is impossible to change the MEX of BBB using the given operations, we define f(B)=0f(B) = 0f(B)=0 instead.

You are given an array AAA of length NNN.

Compute the sum of f(S)f(S)f(S) across all 2N−12^N - 12N−1 non-empty subsets of AAA.

The answer may be large, so report it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the length of the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,…,ANA_1, \ldots, A_NA1​,…,AN​ — the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the sum of f(S)f(S)f(S) across all non-empty subsets SSS of AAA, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The only non-empty subset is {0}\{0\}{0}. The MEX of this subset is 111, and no operations can be performed on it so the MEX cannot change.
Thus, f({0})=0f(\{0\}) = 0f({0})=0 and the answer is 000.
Test case 222: There are three non-empty subsets. Their respective values are:

{0}\{0\}{0} has a value of 000, as seen in the first sample test.
{1}\{1\}{1} has a value of 111, since we can use one operation to decrease 111 to 000, changing the MEX from 000 to 111.
{0,1}\{0, 1\}{0,1} has a value of 111, since we can use one operation to decrease 111 to 000, changing the MEX from 222 to 000.

The answer is hence 0+1+1=20+1+1 = 20+1+1=2.
